name: Loop Body
description: Executes logic for each index in a provided list. Simulates loop body behavior in a single execution pass.
python_code:
import asyncio
async def execute(self, inputs):
    indices = inputs.get('indices')
    if indices is None: indices = []
    
    op = inputs.get('operation', 'Iteration')
    delay = float(inputs.get('delay', 0.0))
    
    self.log_info(f"Starting: {op} for {len(indices)} items.")
    
    # Explicitly set status triggers at start
    self.set_output('completed', False)
    self.set_output('broken', False)
    
    results = []
    is_broken = False
    
    for i in indices:
        # 1. Update UI with current index
        self.set_output('current_index', i)
        self.log_info(f"{op} -> Index: {i}")
        results.append(i)
        
        # 2. Reactive Check for Break
        # We pull the current value of the 'break_condition' port. 
        # If a downstream 'Compare' node is connected, it will have updated this port.
        if bool(self.get_parameter('break_condition', False)):
            self.log_info(f"Break condition met at index {i}!")
            is_broken = True
            break
        
        # 3. Delay
        if delay > 0:
            await asyncio.sleep(delay)
            
    if is_broken:
        self.log_success(f"Loop Broken: {op}")
        self.set_output('broken', True)
    else:
        self.log_success(f"Loop Finished: {op}")
        self.set_output('completed', True)
        
    return {
        'results': results, 
        'current_index': i if indices else None,
        'completed': not is_broken,
        'broken': is_broken
    }
